---
title: "Final Project DA401"
author: "Duc Ngo"
institute: "Denison University"
format: 
  html:
    theme: lux # Check here for more themes: https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-themes.html
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Code"
    code-copy: hover
    link-external-newwindow: true
    tbl-cap-location: top
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3 
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true 

self-contained: true
editor: source
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.align = 'center')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(out.width = '90%')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(results = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.show = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(error = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 1.1, 4.1))

hooks = knitr::knit_hooks$get()
hook_foldable = function(type) {
  force(type)
  function(x, options) {
    res = hooks[[type]](x, options)
    
    if (isFALSE(options[[paste0("fold.", type)]])) return(res)
    
    paste0(
      "<details open><summary>", gsub("^p", "P", gsub("^o", "O", type)), "</summary>\n\n",
      res,
      "\n\n</details>"
    )
  }
}

knitr::knit_hooks$set(
  output = hook_foldable("output"),
  plot = hook_foldable("plot")
)

Q <- 0
```

## Set up

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(zoo)
library(glmnet)
library(randomForest)
library(forecast)
library(knitr)

# File paths
iowa_file <- "D:/Iowa_Liquor_Sales_20260209.csv"
fred_file <- "D:/FRED_data/FRED data.csv"

clean_names_simple <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("([a-z0-9])([A-Z])", "\\1_\\2", x, perl = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", x)
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- gsub("^_+|_+$", "", x)
  x <- gsub("_+", "_", x)
  x
}

# Load raw data
sales_raw <- read_csv(iowa_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
fred_raw  <- read_csv(fred_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Clean column names
names(sales_raw) <- clean_names_simple(names(sales_raw))
names(fred_raw)  <- clean_names_simple(names(fred_raw))

dim(sales_raw)
dim(fred_raw)

names(sales_raw)
names(fred_raw)

head(sales_raw)
head(fred_raw)


```

## Data Cleaning and Processing

```{r}

sales_clean <- sales_raw %>%
  mutate(
    date = mdy(date),
    store_id = store_name,
    city = str_to_upper(city),
    bottles_sold = parse_number(as.character(bottles_sold)),
    sale_dollars = parse_number(as.character(sale_dollars))
  ) %>%
  filter(year(date) >= 2021, year(date) <= 2025)

fred_clean <- fred_raw %>%
  mutate(
    observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)
  ) %>%
  arrange(observation_date)

dim(sales_clean)
dim(fred_clean)

summary(sales_clean$bottles_sold)
summary(sales_clean$sale_dollars)

colSums(is.na(sales_clean))
colSums(is.na(fred_clean))

head(sales_clean)
head(fred_clean)

class(fred_raw$observation_date)
head(fred_raw$observation_date)
```

## Weekly Aggregation

```{r}
weekly_sales <- sales_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(city), !is.na(bottles_sold)) %>%
  filter(bottles_sold >= 0, sale_dollars >= 0) %>%
  mutate(
    week_start = floor_date(date, unit = "week", week_start = 1)
  ) %>%
  group_by(store_id, store_name, city, week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    weekly_bottles = sum(bottles_sold, na.rm = TRUE),
    weekly_sales = sum(sale_dollars, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

dim(weekly_sales)
summary(weekly_sales$weekly_bottles)
summary(weekly_sales$weekly_sales)
head(weekly_sales)

```

## Merge Weekly Sales with FRED Data

```{r}
fred_weekly <- fred_clean %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%
  mutate(
    across(-observation_date, ~ zoo::na.locf(.x, na.rm = FALSE)),
    across(-observation_date, ~ zoo::na.locf(.x, fromLast = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE))
  )

model_df <- weekly_sales %>%
  mutate(
    month_start = floor_date(week_start, unit = "month")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    fred_weekly,
    by = c("month_start" = "observation_date")
  )

# Quick check
dim(model_df)
head(model_df)

colSums(is.na(model_df))

```

## Feature Engineering

```{r}
# Urban vs Rural
urban_cities <- c(
  "DES MOINES", "CEDAR RAPIDS", "DAVENPORT", "SIOUX CITY", "IOWA CITY",
  "WATERLOO", "AMES", "WEST DES MOINES", "ANKENY", "COUNCIL BLUFFS",
  "DUBUQUE", "MARION", "BETTENDORF", "MUSCATINE", "BURLINGTON"
)

# Holiday weeks
holiday_dates <- as.Date(c(
  "2021-01-01", "2021-07-04", "2021-11-25", "2021-12-25",
  "2022-01-01", "2022-07-04", "2022-11-24", "2022-12-25",
  "2023-01-01", "2023-07-04", "2023-11-23", "2023-12-25",
  "2024-01-01", "2024-07-04", "2024-11-28", "2024-12-25",
  "2025-01-01", "2025-07-04", "2025-11-27", "2025-12-25"
))

holiday_weeks <- floor_date(holiday_dates, unit = "week", week_start = 1)

model_df <- model_df %>%
  mutate(
    store_type = if_else(city %in% urban_cities, "Urban", "Rural"),
    is_holiday = if_else(week_start %in% holiday_weeks, 1, 0),
    year = year(week_start),
    month = factor(month.abb[month(week_start)], levels = month.abb),
    week_of_year = isoweek(week_start)
  ) %>%
  arrange(store_id, week_start) %>%   # CHANGED: make time ordering explicit
  group_by(store_id) %>%
  mutate(
    demand_lag1 = lag(weekly_bottles, 1),
    demand_lag2 = lag(weekly_bottles, 2),
    demand_lag4 = lag(weekly_bottles, 4),
    demand_lag12 = lag(weekly_bottles, 12),
    rolling_mean_4 = lag(zoo::rollmean(weekly_bottles, k = 4, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

dim(model_df)
head(model_df)

colSums(is.na(model_df))

```

## Final Model Dataset

```{r}

model_final <- model_df %>%
  drop_na(demand_lag1, demand_lag2, demand_lag4, demand_lag12, rolling_mean_4)

common_test_keys <- model_final %>%
  filter(year == 2025) %>%
  distinct(store_id, week_start)

eligible_stores <- common_test_keys %>%
  distinct(store_id) %>%
  pull(store_id)

nrow(common_test_keys)
length(eligible_stores)

dim(model_final)
head(model_final)

colSums(is.na(model_final))

summary(model_final$weekly_bottles)
summary(model_final$weekly_sales)

```

## Exploratory Data Analysis

```{r}
# EDA dataset from weekly sales
eda_df <- weekly_sales %>%
  mutate(
    store_type = if_else(city %in% urban_cities, "Urban", "Rural"),
    year = year(week_start)
  ) %>%
  filter(week_start >= as.Date("2021-01-04"))

# Table 1: Descriptive statistics by year and store type
desc_table <- eda_df %>%
  group_by(year, store_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_weekly_bottles = mean(weekly_bottles, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_weekly_bottles = median(weekly_bottles, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_weekly_bottles = sd(weekly_bottles, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_weekly_sales = mean(weekly_sales, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

desc_table

```

```{r}
# Figure 1: Total weekly liquor bottles sold
eda_df %>%
  group_by(week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    total_weekly_bottles = sum(weekly_bottles, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = week_start, y = total_weekly_bottles)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 1: Total Weekly Liquor Bottles Sold",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Weekly Bottles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# Figure 2: Weekly bottles sold by store type
eda_df %>%
  group_by(week_start, store_type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_weekly_bottles = sum(weekly_bottles, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = week_start, y = total_weekly_bottles, color = store_type)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 2: Weekly Bottles Sold by Store Type",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Weekly Bottles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
# Figure 3: Correlation heatmap for model variables
corr_data <- model_final %>%
  select(
    weekly_bottles,
    demand_lag1,
    demand_lag2,
    demand_lag4,
    demand_lag12,
    rolling_mean_4,
    cpiaucsl,
    dspic96,
    iaur,
    mrtssm4453_usn,
    payems,
    psavert,
    rsnsr,
    umcsent,
    unrate
  )

corr_mat <- cor(corr_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

corr_df <- as.data.frame(corr_mat) %>%
  rownames_to_column("var1") %>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "corr")

ggplot(corr_df, aes(x = var1, y = var2, fill = corr)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "firebrick", mid = "white", high = "steelblue", midpoint = 0) +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 3: Correlation Heatmap",
    x = "",
    y = "",
    fill = "Correlation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

## Model 1: ARIMA Baseline

```{r}
# Create ARIMA train/test data from weekly sales
arima_train <- weekly_sales %>%
  filter(
    store_id %in% eligible_stores,
    year(week_start) >= 2021,
    year(week_start) <= 2024
  )

arima_test <- weekly_sales %>%
  filter(
    store_id %in% eligible_stores,
    year(week_start) == 2025
  )

dim(arima_train)
dim(arima_test)
```

```{r}
library(forecast)

store_list <- sort(unique(eligible_stores))
arima_pred_list <- list()

for (sid in store_list) {
  
  train_store <- arima_train %>%
    filter(store_id == sid) %>%
    arrange(week_start)
  
  test_store <- arima_test %>%
    filter(store_id == sid) %>%
    arrange(week_start)
  
  if (nrow(train_store) < 20 || nrow(test_store) == 0) next
  
  y_train_store <- train_store$weekly_bottles
  
  fit_arima <- tryCatch(
    auto.arima(
      y_train_store,
      seasonal = FALSE,
      stepwise = TRUE,
      approximation = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  pred_store <- tryCatch(
    {
      if (is.null(fit_arima)) {
        rep(mean(y_train_store, na.rm = TRUE), nrow(test_store))
      } else {
        as.numeric(forecast(fit_arima, h = nrow(test_store))$mean)
      }
    },
    error = function(e) rep(mean(y_train_store, na.rm = TRUE), nrow(test_store))
  )
  
  arima_pred_list[[sid]] <- tibble(
    store_id = sid,
    week_start = test_store$week_start,
    actual = test_store$weekly_bottles,
    pred_arima = pmax(pred_store, 0)
  )
}

```


```{r}
# Combine predictions
arima_results <- bind_rows(arima_pred_list) %>%
  semi_join(common_test_keys, by = c("store_id", "week_start"))

dim(arima_results)
head(arima_results)



```


```{r}
# Test RMSE
rmse_arima <- sqrt(mean((arima_results$actual - arima_results$pred_arima)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
rmse_arima


```


```{r}
# Test MAE
mae_arima <- mean(abs(arima_results$actual - arima_results$pred_arima), na.rm = TRUE)
mae_arima

```

```{r}
# Plot actual vs predicted totals in 2025
arima_results %>%
  group_by(week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    actual = sum(actual, na.rm = TRUE),
    pred_arima = sum(pred_arima, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-week_start, names_to = "series", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = week_start, y = value, color = series)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 4: ARIMA: Actual vs Predicted Weekly Demand in 2025",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Weekly Bottles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```




## Data Partitioning

```{r}
library(caret)

# Make sure factors are coded correctly
model_final <- model_final %>%
  mutate(
    store_type = factor(store_type),
    month = factor(month, levels = month.abb)
  )

# Time-based train/test split
train_data <- model_final %>%
  filter(year >= 2021, year <= 2024)

test_data <- model_final %>%
  filter(year == 2025)

dim(train_data)
dim(test_data)

table(train_data$year)
table(test_data$year)

stopifnot(max(train_data$week_start) < min(test_data$week_start))
stopifnot(
  nrow(test_data %>% distinct(store_id, week_start)) ==
    nrow(common_test_keys)
)

```



```{r}
# Create x and y for regularization models
x_train <- model.matrix(
  weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
    demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
    cpiaucsl + cusr0000_saf112 + cusr0000_saf116 + dspic96 + iaur +
    mrtssm4453_usn + payems + psavert + rsnsr + umcsent + unrate,
  data = train_data
)[, -1]

y_train <- train_data$weekly_bottles

x_test <- model.matrix(
  weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
    demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
    cpiaucsl + cusr0000_saf112 + cusr0000_saf116 + dspic96 + iaur +
    mrtssm4453_usn + payems + psavert + rsnsr + umcsent + unrate,
  data = test_data
)[, -1]

y_test <- test_data$weekly_bottles

dim(x_train)
dim(x_test)

length(y_train)
length(y_test)

```

```{r}
## Model 2: Lasso Regression

library(glmnet)

set.seed(123)

# Cross-validation to choose lambda
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(
  x_train,
  y_train,
  alpha = 1
)

# Best lambda
best_lam_lasso <- cv_lasso$lambda.min
best_lam_lasso

# Plot CV curve
plot(cv_lasso)

```


```{r}

# Fit lasso model on the full training set using best lambda
lasso_fit <- glmnet(
  x_train,
  y_train,
  alpha = 1,
  lambda = best_lam_lasso
)

# Predict on test set
lasso_pred <- predict(
  lasso_fit,
  s = best_lam_lasso,
  newx = x_test
)

# Test MSE
lasso_mse <- mean((y_test - lasso_pred)^2)
lasso_mse

```

```{r}
# Test RMSE
rmse_lasso <- sqrt(mean((y_test - lasso_pred)^2))
rmse_lasso

# Test MAE
mae_lasso <- mean(abs(y_test - lasso_pred))
mae_lasso

# Coefficients
coef(lasso_fit)
```

```{r}
# Put coefficients into a cleaner table
lasso_coef <- as.matrix(coef(lasso_fit)) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("variable") %>%
  rename(coefficient = s0) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

head(lasso_coef, 20)

```

```{r}
# Simple results table
lasso_results <- data.frame(
  Model = "Lasso",
  MSE = lasso_mse,
  RMSE = rmse_lasso,
  MAE = mae_lasso,
  Best_Lambda = best_lam_lasso
)

lasso_results

```

## Model 3: Random Forest

```{r}
library(randomForest)

set.seed(123)

# Sample training data for faster random forest
train_rf <- train_data %>%
  sample_n(20000)

# Smaller tuning grid
mtry_grid <- c(4, 8)

rf_tuning <- data.frame(
  mtry = mtry_grid,
  oob_mse = NA
)

for (i in 1:length(mtry_grid)) {
  
  rf_temp <- randomForest(
    weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
      demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
      cpiaucsl + cusr0000_saf112 + cusr0000_saf116 + dspic96 + iaur +
      mrtssm4453_usn + payems + psavert + rsnsr + umcsent + unrate,
    data = train_rf,
    ntree = 20,
    mtry = mtry_grid[i],
    importance = FALSE
  )
  
  rf_tuning$oob_mse[i] <- tail(rf_temp$mse, 1)
}

rf_tuning

best_mtry <- rf_tuning$mtry[which.min(rf_tuning$oob_mse)]
best_mtry

# Final random forest model
rf_fit <- randomForest(
  weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
    demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
    cpiaucsl + cusr0000_saf112 + cusr0000_saf116 + dspic96 + iaur +
    mrtssm4453_usn + payems + psavert + rsnsr + umcsent + unrate,
  data = train_rf,
  ntree = 50,
  mtry = best_mtry,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_fit

# Predict on test set
rf_pred <- predict(rf_fit, newdata = test_data)

rf_mse <- mean((y_test - rf_pred)^2)
rmse_rf <- sqrt(mean((y_test - rf_pred)^2))
mae_rf <- mean(abs(y_test - rf_pred))

rf_results <- data.frame(
  Model = "Random Forest",
  MSE = rf_mse,
  RMSE = rmse_rf,
  MAE = mae_rf,
  Best_mtry = best_mtry
)

rf_results

# Variable importance
importance(rf_fit)
varImpPlot(rf_fit)


```

```{r}



library(tidyverse)

# 1. Get permutation importance from random forest
rf_imp <- importance(rf_fit, type = 1) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("variable") %>%
  rename(perm_importance = `%IncMSE`) %>%
  arrange(desc(perm_importance))

# 2. Rename variables for plotting
rf_imp <- rf_imp %>%
  mutate(
    variable_label = recode(
      variable,
      demand_lag1 = "Demand lag (1 week)",
      demand_lag2 = "Demand lag (2 weeks)",
      demand_lag4 = "Demand lag (4 weeks)",
      demand_lag12 = "Demand lag (12 weeks)",
      rolling_mean_4 = "Rolling mean (4 weeks)",
      cpiaucsl = "CPI",
      cusr0000_saf112 = "Alcohol CPI 1",
      cusr0000_saf116 = "Alcohol CPI 2",
      dspic96 = "Real disposable income",
      iaur = "Iowa unemployment rate",
      mrtssm4453_usn = "Liquor retail sales",
      payems = "Total employment",
      psavert = "Personal saving rate",
      rsnsr = "Recession indicator",
      umcsent = "Consumer sentiment",
      unrate = "Unemployment rate",
      week_of_year = "Week of year",
      store_type = "Store type",
      month = "Month",
      is_holiday = "Holiday week"
    )
  )

# 3. Keep top 10 predictors and highlight top 5
rf_top10 <- rf_imp %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    highlight = if_else(row_number() <= 5, "Top 5", "Other"),
    variable_label = factor(variable_label, levels = rev(variable_label))
  )

# 4. Plot
ggplot(rf_top10, aes(x = perm_importance, y = variable_label, fill = highlight)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(perm_importance, 1)), hjust = -0.15, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Top 5" = "#aa2b1e", "Other" = "grey60")) +
  labs(
    title = "Random Forest: Top 10 Predictors",
    x = "% Increase in MSE when feature is permuted",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(rf_top10$perm_importance) * 1.15)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )


```

# Model Evaluation

## Model Comparison

```{r}
# Combine model results into one table
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("ARIMA", "Lasso", "Random Forest"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_arima, rmse_lasso, rmse_rf),
  MAE  = c(mae_arima, mae_lasso, mae_rf)
) %>%
  arrange(RMSE)

comparison_table


```

```{r}
# Build prediction datasets for each model
lasso_pred_df <- test_data %>%
  select(store_id, week_start, actual = weekly_bottles) %>%
  mutate(pred_lasso = as.numeric(lasso_pred))

rf_pred_df <- test_data %>%
  select(store_id, week_start, actual = weekly_bottles) %>%
  mutate(pred_rf = as.numeric(rf_pred))

# Combine all predictions
all_preds <- test_data %>%
  select(store_id, week_start, actual = weekly_bottles) %>%
  left_join(
    arima_results %>% select(store_id, week_start, pred_arima),
    by = c("store_id", "week_start")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    lasso_pred_df %>% select(store_id, week_start, pred_lasso),
    by = c("store_id", "week_start")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    rf_pred_df %>% select(store_id, week_start, pred_rf),
    by = c("store_id", "week_start")
  )

head(all_preds)

```

```{r}


# Aggregate to weekly totals for plotting
plot_df <- all_preds %>%
  group_by(week_start) %>%
  summarise(
    actual = sum(actual, na.rm = TRUE),
    arima = sum(pred_arima, na.rm = TRUE),
    lasso = sum(pred_lasso, na.rm = TRUE),
    rf = sum(pred_rf, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-week_start, names_to = "series", values_to = "value")

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = week_start, y = value, color = series)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 4: Actual vs Predicted Weekly Demand in 2025",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Total Weekly Bottles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Robustness Check: Reduced Macro Set

```{r}
# Build x matrices with a smaller macro set
x_train_small <- model.matrix(
  weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
    demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
    cpiaucsl + iaur + payems + umcsent + unrate,
  data = train_data
)[, -1]

x_test_small <- model.matrix(
  weekly_bottles ~ store_type + month + week_of_year + is_holiday +
    demand_lag1 + demand_lag2 + demand_lag4 + demand_lag12 + rolling_mean_4 +
    cpiaucsl + iaur + payems + umcsent + unrate,
  data = test_data
)[, -1]

# Fit reduced-macro Lasso
set.seed(123)

cv_lasso_small <- cv.glmnet(
  x_train_small,
  y_train,
  alpha = 1
)

best_lam_lasso_small <- cv_lasso_small$lambda.min
best_lam_lasso_small

lasso_small_fit <- glmnet(
  x_train_small,
  y_train,
  alpha = 1,
  lambda = best_lam_lasso_small
)

lasso_small_pred <- predict(
  lasso_small_fit,
  s = best_lam_lasso_small,
  newx = x_test_small
)

# Evaluation
rmse_lasso_small <- sqrt(mean((y_test - lasso_small_pred)^2))
mae_lasso_small  <- mean(abs(y_test - lasso_small_pred))

rmse_lasso_small
mae_lasso_small

```


```{r}
# Compare main Lasso vs reduced-macro Lasso
robustness_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Main Lasso", "Reduced-macro Lasso"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_lasso, rmse_lasso_small),
  MAE  = c(mae_lasso, mae_lasso_small)
)

robustness_table


```
